article_title: Astranis Closes $450 Million Series E and Credit Facility at $2.8 Billion Valuation to Scale Small-GEO Satellite Production
summary: Substantively sound funding piece. Program-of-Record names attributed to SpaceNews but not in the snapshot (likely from consent-gated Yahoo press release); other specifics fully verified.

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: consent.yahoo.com
PTS-G / Resilient GPS / Andromeda attributed to SpaceNews but not in snapshot
findings.details: The three Program-of-Record names are attributed to SpaceNews but those strings do not appear in the SpaceNews snapshot. They likely come from the Yahoo Finance press release, which is consent-gated and unverifiable from snapshot. The substance is consistent with the verbatim Gedmark quote 'spooling up to support multiple US Government programs of record simultaneously' in SpaceNews.

content_preview.title: Astranis Closes $450 Million Series E and Credit Facility at $2.8 Billion Valuation to Scale Small-GEO Satellite Production
content_preview.summary: Astranis raised $300M Series E plus $155M Trinity Capital credit facility at a $2.8B valuation, scaling micro-GEO satellite output for U.S. defense Programs of Record and sovereign telecom customers in Taiwan and Oman.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

San Francisco-based satellite startup Astranis announced on May 6, 2026, that it has closed $450 million in new capital, comprising a $300 million Series E equity round and a $155 million delayed-draw credit facility from Trinity Capital. The combined raise values the company at $2.8 billion and brings its lifetime funding above $1.2 billion, [according to SpaceNews](https://spacenews.com/astranis-secures-450-million-in-equity-debt-to-expand-small-geo-satellite-production/). The cap...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong 860-word funding-announcement piece. Clean structure, hedged 'What We Don't Know', proper analysis of defense-tech market.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read both snapshots. SpaceNews (source-0) confirms verbatim: $450M total / $300M Series E / $155M Trinity Capital delayed-draw / $2.8B valuation, Snowpoint Ventures + Franklin Templeton co-lead, full investor list (Andreessen Horowitz / BlackRock / Baillie Gifford / Fidelity / BAM Elevate / Nimble Partners / Friends & Family Capital), May 6 announcement, John Gedmark CEO + co-founder, 'spooling up to support multiple U.S. government programs of record simultaneously' verbatim, ~500 employees, 153,000-square-foot Northern California facility, Chunghwa Telecom + MB Group of Oman customers, five satellites on orbit, micro-GEO classification, Alexander Creasey 'single most important orbit for national security' attribution. Yahoo Finance (source-1) redirected to consent.yahoo.com — most of the Yahoo-attributed press release content is unverifiable from snapshot. The PTS-G / Resilient GPS / Andromeda specific Program-of-Record names attributed to SpaceNews are not in the SpaceNews snapshot; they likely come from the Yahoo press release.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Headline + lead claims fully verified. The three Program-of-Record names attributed to SpaceNews are unverifiable from snapshot (they are plausibly real but should have been attributed to the Yahoo press release, which is the canonical primary source).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Properly hedged on government-revenue concentration and valuation downside.
editor_notes.originality: First-of-cluster — PR #1216 covers the same Astranis announcement and will be rejected as duplicate downstream.
concerns: Program-of-Record names (PTS-G, Resilient GPS, Andromeda) attributed to SpaceNews but not in SpaceNews snapshot.
recommendations: Future submissions: if a fact only appears in the consent-gated source, attribute it to that source rather than re-attributing it to a different cleaner source.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively sound. Misattributed PoR names are a single recoverable issue — substance is real and consistent with the Gedmark quote that IS verified verbatim.
